qa_pairs.question: What happens after selecting a job template in Open Composer?
qa_pairs.answer: After selecting a job template, you will be taken to the job submission page, which is split between job parameters on the left and the job script itself on the right.

qa_pairs.question: What kind of resources can you control using the job parameters on the job submission page?
qa_pairs.answer: The job parameters allow you to control resources such as the number of nodes, number of tasks per node, wall clock time, and the output file name.

qa_pairs.question: Can I directly edit the job script in Open Composer?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, you may edit the job script directly if you wish, and any changes made to the job parameters will be reflected in the job script automatically.

qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of the 'Script Location' field in Open Composer?
qa_pairs.answer: The 'Script Location' field specifies the directory where the job script will be saved and where your job will be run from.

qa_pairs.question: What does the 'Script Name' field define on the job submission page?
qa_pairs.answer: The 'Script Name' field specifies the name of the job script file.

qa_pairs.question: What is the 'Job Name' field used for in Open Composer?
qa_pairs.answer: The 'Job Name' field specifies the name of the job that will appear in the job queue.

qa_pairs.question: How can I change the cluster for my job submission in Open Composer?
qa_pairs.answer: You can change the cluster by using the 'Cluster' field, allowing you to choose between options like Trillium (default) or Trillium-GPU.

qa_pairs.question: What additional parameter becomes available when 'Trillium-GPU' is selected as the cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: Selecting 'Trillium-GPU' will provide an additional job parameter to request GPU resources.

qa_pairs.question: How do you submit a job to the scheduler in Open Composer?
qa_pairs.answer: Once you are satisfied with your job script, click on the 'Submit' button to submit the job to the scheduler and save your script to the 'Script Location'.

qa_pairs.question: What indicates a successful job submission in Open Composer?
qa_pairs.answer: If your job was submitted successfully, you will see a confirmation message at the top of the page with your job ID.

qa_pairs.question: Are the template scripts provided in Open Composer ready to use without modifications?
qa_pairs.answer: No, the template scripts provided in Open Composer are basic examples to get you started, and you will need to modify them further to suit your specific needs.

qa_pairs.question: What kind of modifications might be necessary for a job script in Open Composer?
qa_pairs.answer: You may need to modify the job script to load required modules and specify input/output files.

qa_pairs.question: What limits must job scripts conform to in Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: Job scripts still need to conform to the limits set by the Trillium Slurm scheduler.

qa_pairs.question: Where can users find more information on writing job scripts for Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: Users should refer to the Trillium documentation for more information on how to write job scripts.

qa_pairs.question: How can I monitor my submitted jobs in Open Composer?
qa_pairs.answer: To monitor your submitted jobs in Open Composer, navigate to the 'History' tab.

qa_pairs.question: What information is displayed in the 'History' tab for submitted jobs?
qa_pairs.answer: The 'History' tab displays a list of all your submitted jobs, along with their status: Queued, Running, Completed, or Failed.

qa_pairs.question: How can jobs in the 'History' tab be filtered?
qa_pairs.answer: You can filter jobs by using the 'Filter' text box at the top right or by using the checkboxes below the filter.

qa_pairs.question: What information does clicking on the 'Job ID' column field provide?
qa_pairs.answer: Clicking on 'Job ID' opens the job in my.SciNet, which displays performance statistics and more detailed Slurm information about the job.

qa_pairs.question: What happens if a job has not started or was cancelled when viewed in my.SciNet?
qa_pairs.answer: my.SciNet may show 'Not found or not permitted' if the job hasn't started yet or was cancelled.

qa_pairs.question: What does clicking on the 'Application' column field in the 'History' tab do?
qa_pairs.answer: Clicking on 'Application' opens the job script editor of the template you used.

qa_pairs.question: What happens when you click on the 'Script Location' column field in Open Composer's 'History' tab?
qa_pairs.answer: Clicking on 'Script Location' opens an OOD file browser window at the location of the job script, and a small terminal icon allows you to open a terminal in that location.

qa_pairs.question: What is displayed when you click on the 'Script Name' column field?
qa_pairs.answer: Clicking on 'Script Name' displays the job script that was submitted to the scheduler.

qa_pairs.question: How can a previously run job be resubmitted or modified in Open Composer?
qa_pairs.answer: To resubmit or modify a previously run job, click on the job script under the 'Script Name' column and then click 'Load Parameters'. This will take you back to the job submission page for further modifications.

qa_pairs.question: What applications does Open Composer currently support?
qa_pairs.answer: Open Composer currently supports MPI Slurm Job, OpenMP Slurm Job, Hybrid MPI/OpenMP Slurm Job, Python Slurm Job, R Slurm Job, and VASP Slurm Job.

qa_pairs.question: How can I get an overview of all my jobs in the queue?
qa_pairs.answer: You can get an overview of all your jobs in the queue by navigating to the 'Jobs' tab and selecting 'Active Jobs'.

qa_pairs.question: How can jobs be filtered in the 'Active Jobs' interface?
qa_pairs.answer: Jobs can be filtered by using the 'Filter' text box at the top right of the 'Active Jobs' interface.

qa_pairs.question: How can columns be sorted in the 'Active Jobs' interface?
qa_pairs.answer: Columns can be sorted by clicking on the column headers, for example, by job status (running, completed, failed, etc.).

qa_pairs.question: What additional details are available when clicking '>' next to a job in the 'Active Jobs' interface?
qa_pairs.answer: Clicking on '>' to the left of a job will show more details, such as the start/end time, node list, and account charged.

qa_pairs.question: How can I view all jobs in the queue, not just active ones?
qa_pairs.answer: You can view all jobs in the queue by clicking on the drop-down menu at the top right of the 'Active Jobs' interface and selecting 'All Jobs'.

qa_pairs.question: Where can a more detailed view of jobs be found?
qa_pairs.answer: A more detailed view of your jobs can still be found using the my.SciNet portal.
